title: Nature & Places

examples.kp: Maliwanag ya ing batuin king banua.
examples.en: The star is bright in the sky.
There are many stars at night.
The rain is heavy today.
It might rain later.

entries.notes: Common weather term; kauran refers to the rainy season.

examples.en: The wind is strong today.
The wind is cool.
The clouds on the mountain are thick.

examples.kp: Mekublan ne ing aldo ning ulap.
examples.en: The sun was covered by cloud.

entries.notes: In Kapampangan, ulap can refer to clouds, mist, or fog. Cognate with Tagalog ulap (cloud) and Ilocano ulep (cloud).

examples.en: The fire is big.
There is fire here, let us be careful.

entries.notes: Often refers to hearth fire in domestic contexts.

examples.en: Mount Arayat is tall.
I want to go to the mountain.

entries.notes: Documented as early as 1621 in Fray Francisco Coronel's Arte y Reglas de la Lengua Pampanga.

examples.en: The river here is wide.

examples.kp: Ding ilug palwal la king dayat malat.
examples.en: The rivers flow out to the sea.
The sea is far from here.

examples.kp: Ding ilug palwal la king dagat.
examples.en: The rivers flow out to the sea.

entries.notes: Used in the Kapampangan Bible (PmPV, Genesis 1). The older/traditional term is dayat malat (literally 'salty field'); the Tagalog word dagat is a borrowing and corruption of Kapampangan dayat. Both forms are current in Kapampangan.

examples.en: The tree there is big.
Ipil-ipil trees are durable wood.
The flower is beautiful.
There are many flowers in the garden.

entries.notes: The general Kapampangan word for flower. Also the name of the sampaguita jasmine, a culturally significant flower used in garlands for ceremonies.

examples.kp: Malutu ya ing bulung ning panaun.
examples.en: The leaf turned red in the season.
There are many leaves on the tree.
The road there is rocky.
There are many stones in the river.

examples.kp: Manyaman ya ing gabun keni para king tanaman.
examples.en: The soil here is good for planting.
It was dropped to the ground.

examples.kp: Malapit ya ing dalan king balen.
examples.en: The road to the town is near.

examples.kp: Malupit ya ing dalan papunta king bunduk.
examples.en: The path to the mountain is rough.

examples.kp: Masanting a balen ya ing Angeles.
examples.en: Angeles is a fine town.
It is the town of the Kapampangan people.
I am going to the market today.

examples.kp: Malapit ya ing palengke king balen.
examples.en: The market is near the town.

entries.notes: Shared with Tagalog; from Spanish palenque.

examples.kp: Maragul ya ing pisamban king balen.
examples.en: The church in town is large.
I go to church on Sunday.

entries.notes: The traditional Kapampangan word for church, distinct from the Tagalog simbahan.

examples.en: I am going to school.
The school is far from here.

lessons.title: Sky & Weather
lessons.intro: Learn words for what you see and feel in the sky: stars, rain, wind, and clouds.

lessons.intro: The Kapampangan homeland is shaped by rivers flowing to the sea. Learn these essential water words.

lessons.title: Community Places
lessons.intro: Every Kapampangan town has a market, church, and school. Learn the words for these important community places.

sources: Forman, Michael L. (1971). Kapampangan Dictionary. University of Hawaii Press. (Entry: dayat-malat = ocean; bunduk = mountain; ilug = river)
Coronel, Francisco (1621). Arte y Reglas de la Lengua Pampanga. (Documents bunduk and ilug in the 17th century)
Ing Mayap a Balita Biblia (PmPV), Genesis 1 via YouVersion — https://www.bible.com/bible/1141/GEN.1.PMPV (batuin, dagat/dayat, gabun, banua, api)
tagaloglang.com Kapampangan Dictionary — https://www.tagaloglang.com/kapampangan-dictionary/ (uran, angin, ulap, balen, pisamban, gabun)

Common Kapampangan Words & Phrases (idoc.pub) — https://idoc.pub/documents/common-kapampangan-words-phrasesdocx-vlr9gpp65jlz (uran, angin, ulap, api, dalan, balen, pisamban, iskuwela, sampaga, punu/dutung, batuin)
Learn Kapampangan on X (@kapdaily) — https://x.com/kapdaily/status/5112534030 (sampaga = flower, vs. Tagalog bulaklak)
ivanhenares.com: Pisamban – the churches of Pampanga — https://www.ivanhenares.com/2006/07/pisamban-churches-of-pampanga.html (pisamban = church)
The Kapampangan Listorian — https://kaplistorian.blogspot.com/2016/07/3-10-most-beautiful-words-of.html (dayat, bunduk Arayat context)
SunStar Pampanga / Garcia columns — multiple articles confirming uran, angin, ilug, gabun, bunduk in running Kapampangan text
confidenceNotes: High confidence: batuin (star), uran (rain), angin (wind), api (fire), bunduk (mountain), ilug (river), batu (stone), gabun (soil), dalan (road), balen (town), palengke (market), pisamban (church), iskuwela (school), sampaga (flower), dutung (tree), bulung (leaf) — all corroborated by multiple independent sources including Wiktionary, Forman's dictionary, colonial-era grammar, and native Kapampangan text corpora. Moderate confidence: ulap (cloud/mist) — attested in multiple vocabulary lists as the Kapampangan equivalent of Tagalog ulap; Kaikki/Wiktionary notes it primarily means fog/mist in Kapampangan but it is used for clouds in common vocabulary resources. Moderate confidence: dayat malat (sea) — the two-word phrase is confirmed in Forman's dictionary; dayat alone means irrigated rice field, so the full phrase dayat malat is used as the entry headword to avoid ambiguity.
